GoHighLevel Agency Pricing: What to Charge Clients in 2026

GoHighLevel pricing for agencies: platform plan costs ($97-$497), white label SaaS fees and markup strategies to price your own clients profitably. Includes a pricing model comparison and agency ROI framework. Updated 2026.

TL;DR

  • GoHighLevel agencies typically charge clients $297-$997/mo depending on the service package
  • SaaS Mode resellers commonly price at $97-$497/mo per sub-account seat
  • HVAC, roofing, legal and dental are the highest-paying niches — retainers of $500-$2,000/mo are normal
  • Your GHL platform cost is $97-$497/mo total — you cover that at 1-2 client accounts
  • Profit starts at client #3 and compounds with every account you add
  • Usage fees (SMS, phone, AI) should be rebilled to clients via SaaS Mode wallet top-ups

✅ HighLevel 30-Day Free Trial

Claim Your Free Month

Who This Is For

  • Agencies setting up GoHighLevel for the first time and figuring out how to price
  • Freelancers packaging GHL services as retainer offers
  • SaaS Mode operators setting their monthly seat price
  • Business owners curious what agencies are actually charging for this platform

Why Most Agencies Underprice GHL Services

The most common mistake: pricing GoHighLevel by the tool, not the outcome.

If you tell a client "I'll set up your CRM for $97/mo," you are competing with a software subscription. If you say "I'll set up automated appointment booking, lead follow-up, and reputation management that generates 30% more booked jobs," you are selling a result.

Same platform. Different pricing power.

GoHighLevel replaces tools that most service businesses are already paying for — separately, poorly integrated, and without the automation layer. The average local business is spending $400-$800/mo on disconnected tools that do half of what GoHighLevel does in one place. Your retainer should be priced against that replacement value, not against the raw platform cost.

Skip trial and error.

Follow a proven onboarding sequence used by agencies.

Start the HighLevel Bootcamp

GoHighLevel Platform Costs (Your Overhead)

Before setting client prices, know your costs.

PlanMonthly CostBest For
Starter$97/mo1-3 client sub-accounts, basic delivery
Unlimited$297/mo4+ clients, white-label optional
Agency Pro (SaaS Mode)$497/moReselling seats, full white-label brand

Add on average:

  • $30-$100/mo per active client in LC Phone + LC Email usage
  • $497/mo if you want a white-label mobile app on the Unlimited plan
  • $0.05-$0.12 per AI Employee conversation if activated for clients

At Unlimited ($297/mo), two full-service retainer clients at $297/mo each cover your platform. Everything after that is margin.

Full plan breakdown: GoHighLevel Pricing 2026 — $97, $297 or $497.

Agency Retainer Pricing by Service Type

Done-For-You (DFY) Monthly Retainer

You manage the platform. Client pays monthly for results.

Service PackageWhat's IncludedTypical Monthly Retainer
Starter CRM SetupPipeline, contact import, basic automation$297-$497
Lead Nurture SystemSMS + email follow-up workflows, lead scoring$497-$797
Full Agency DeliveryCRM + automation + funnels + reputation$797-$1,497
AI-Enabled PackageAbove + AI Employee + Voice AI setup$1,200-$2,500

SaaS Seat Resale (Done-With-You / White-Label)

You give clients access to a branded sub-account and charge a monthly seat fee.

SaaS Seat TierWhat Client GetsTypical Seat Price
BasicCRM, pipelines, email automation$97-$147/mo
StandardAbove + SMS, booking, reputation$197-$297/mo
ProAbove + funnels, AI Employee, advanced workflows$297-$497/mo

At 10 clients on a $297/mo seat, your MRR from that one tier is $2,970. Platform cost: $497/mo. Gross margin: $2,473/mo before usage fees.

At 20 clients: $5,940/mo MRR. Platform cost still $497/mo. That is the SaaS Mode model in practice.

Full SaaS Mode profit walkthrough: GoHighLevel SaaS Mode Complete Guide.

Retainer Ranges by Niche (2026)

These are based on what the market supports for each industry vertical.

NicheMinimum Viable RetainerStrong PackagePremium (AI + Full Automation)
HVAC$497/mo$797/mo$1,200/mo
Roofing$497/mo$997/mo$1,500/mo
General Contractor$397/mo$697/mo$1,000/mo
Dental Practice$597/mo$997/mo$1,500/mo
Law Firm$797/mo$1,497/mo$2,500/mo
Real Estate Agent$297/mo$497/mo$797/mo
Mortgage Broker$397/mo$697/mo$1,200/mo
Med Spa$597/mo$997/mo$1,500/mo
Auto Dealership$497/mo$997/mo$1,500/mo
Restaurant$197/mo$397/mo$597/mo
Fitness Studio$297/mo$497/mo$797/mo
Massage Therapist$197/mo$297/mo$497/mo

Why some niches pay more:

High-revenue, high-stakes decisions (legal, dental, home services) have high LTV per client. An agency that generates 3 extra booked cases/month for a law firm at $2,000/case in fees can easily justify a $1,500/mo retainer. Price to the value created, not the hours worked.

The Rebilling Model: Don't Absorb Usage Costs

A mistake agencies make: paying usage fees out of their own margin.

GoHighLevel SaaS Mode includes a wallet system. You can top up client wallets with credits for LC Phone, LC Email and AI conversations. Clients use their wallet balance, you refill it — either at cost or with markup.

Common rebilling approaches:

MethodHow It WorksYour Margin
Pass-throughClient prepays wallet, you refill at cost$0 on usage, margin only on retainer
20% markupYou charge $120 for $100 of usage credits20% on all usage
Usage bundleInclude $50/mo of usage in package priceFlat — you eat overages or cap them

The cleanest model for beginners: bundle $50-$100/mo of usage into the retainer price and document it. Clients understand "this covers your SMS and email." If they run over, top up at cost.

The March 2026 usage export update now includes rebilling status per sub-account in the Costs sheet — making it faster to catch which accounts have rebilling off and are absorbing agency costs.

Revenue Model Example: 10-Client Agency

Scenario: Agency Pro plan ($497/mo), 10 clients on a $497/mo full-delivery retainer.

Line ItemMonthly
Gross revenue (10 × $497)$4,970
GHL platform cost$497
Usage fees (estimated $60/client)$600
Net before your time$3,873/mo

Add 5 more SaaS-only seats at $197/mo:

Additional LineMonthly
SaaS seat revenue (5 × $197)$985
No additional platform cost$0
Usage fees (estimated $20/client)$100
Net addition$885/mo

Combined gross: $5,955/mo. Combined platform overhead: still $497/mo.

This is why GoHighLevel agencies scale differently from hourly agencies. The platform cost does not increase as revenue increases.

What to Include in Each Package Tier

$97-$197/mo SaaS Seat (software access only)

  • Branded sub-account
  • Pre-built snapshot deployed
  • Self-serve training (HighLevel University link)
  • Email support only

$297-$497/mo Standard Retainer

  • Everything above
  • Monthly automation review
  • Lead nurture sequences active
  • SMS follow-up configured
  • Reputation management running
  • Monthly report

$797-$1,500/mo Full-Service Retainer

  • Everything above
  • AI Employee configured and trained on their business
  • Funnel built and tested
  • Ad integration if running paid traffic
  • Bi-weekly check-in call
  • Priority support

$1,500+/mo Premium (AI-First Package)

  • Everything above
  • Voice AI set up for inbound calls
  • Custom GPT or AI Agent trained on their FAQs and offers
  • Monthly strategy session
  • Competitor monitoring
  • Multi-location if applicable

How to Justify Your Price to a Client

Clients rarely push back on price when the value is quantified. Use this conversation:

"Right now you're paying [X tools] at [Y total/mo]. They don't talk to each other, nothing is automated, and you're following up manually or not at all. Every missed lead at your close rate is worth [LTV]. We will automate the follow-up, book appointments and manage your reputation — all in one place, under your brand. The platform pays for itself if it books one additional appointment per month."

For industries with a high average client value (legal, dental, HVAC), a single additional booking easily exceeds a $500/mo retainer. That is the math that closes the deal.

FAQ

What should I charge for a GoHighLevel retainer? Start at $297-$497/mo for a basic CRM and automation setup. Move to $797-$1,500/mo for full-service delivery with AI Employee and active campaign management. The right price depends on the niche — high-LTV businesses like law firms and dental practices support higher retainers.

Can I charge clients separately for SMS and email usage? Yes. Use GoHighLevel SaaS Mode wallet rebilling. You can pass usage through at cost or add a markup. The cleaner option for most agencies is to bundle $50-$100 of usage into the monthly retainer and manage it from there.

What do most GoHighLevel agencies charge per month? Community data and Reddit threads suggest the typical range is $300-$1,000/mo for full-service retainers and $97-$297/mo for SaaS seat resale. Agencies in high-value niches (legal, dental, roofing) regularly charge $1,000-$2,500/mo.

How many clients do I need to break even on GoHighLevel? At the Agency Pro plan ($497/mo), you break even with 2 clients paying $297/mo or 1 client paying $497/mo. Profit starts at client #3. The math accelerates because platform cost is fixed.

What is SaaS Mode and how does it affect pricing? SaaS Mode (Agency Pro at $497/mo) lets you resell GoHighLevel sub-accounts under your own brand at a monthly fee. You set the price per seat. Clients see your brand, not GoHighLevel's. This turns your agency platform into a recurring SaaS revenue stream.

Should I charge a setup fee? Yes. One-time setup fees of $500-$3,000 are standard depending on complexity (snapshot deployment, custom workflow builds, funnel creation, integration setup). Setup fees are not optional — they protect your time and set expectations about the work involved.

What is the average monthly retainer for a GoHighLevel HVAC agency? HVAC retainers typically run $497-$1,200/mo depending on the service scope. A basic automation and follow-up setup is $497/mo. A full AI Employee + reputation + ad integration package is $1,000-$1,500/mo.

Start With a 30-Day Trial

If you are setting up GoHighLevel for the first time to build your agency offer, the extended trial gives you 30 days to configure your snapshot, test your automation and onboard a pilot client before committing.

✅ GoHighLevel 30-Day Free Trial
